finding: Do not use imaging severity alone to trigger surgery.
title: Image-only surgery boundary
why_it_matters: Surgical discussion requires symptoms, function, imaging, preference, and clinician review.
recommended_action: Route to clinician-led orthopaedic evaluation if burden remains high.

finding: Do not start routine oral NSAID without renal review.
title: Renal gate for oral NSAID
why_it_matters: Renal status changes NSAID risk and monitoring.
recommended_action: Collect eGFR or creatinine before oral NSAID decisions.

finding: Do not start routine oral NSAID without gastrointestinal risk review.
title: Gastrointestinal gate for oral NSAID
why_it_matters: Ulcer or bleeding history can change drug safety.
recommended_action: Collect GI history and gastroprotection plan if needed.

finding: Do not start routine oral NSAID without cardiovascular and blood pressure review.
title: Cardiovascular gate for oral NSAID
why_it_matters: Blood pressure and CV risk can outweigh analgesic benefit.
recommended_action: Review CV history, BP, and interacting drugs.

finding: Do not start routine oral NSAID without current-medication review.
title: Medication reconciliation
why_it_matters: Anticoagulants, antiplatelets, and duplicate analgesics change risk.

finding: Use injection only as a short-term bridge when pain or effusion blocks rehabilitation.
title: Injection boundary
why_it_matters: Avoid routine repeated injection without reassessment.
recommended_action: Use clinician-led flare bridge only if indicated.

finding: Do not repeat injection without reassessment.
title: Repeated injection review
why_it_matters: Repeated procedures can mask inadequate escalation planning.

finding: Avoid high-impact loading in the target knee.
title: High-impact loading
why_it_matters: High-impact activity can worsen pain or fall risk in advanced disease.
recommended_action: Use low-impact aerobic plan and graded strengthening.

finding: Stop or reduce activity if pain rises more than 2 points for over 24 hours.
title: Exercise stop rule
why_it_matters: Stop rules make rehabilitation safer and executable.

finding: Escalate urgently for fever, acute red swollen joint, trauma, locking, or sudden decline.
title: Urgent red flags
why_it_matters: Red flags require direct clinical evaluation.

finding: Include fall-prevention review when fall history or balance issue is present.
title: Fall-prevention review
why_it_matters: Falls change exercise prescription and home safety needs.
recommended_action: Add balance training and assistive-device review.

finding: Keep contralateral findings as comparison, not target-knee labels.
title: Side-label protection
why_it_matters: Side-label mistakes can invalidate image-linked planning.
recommended_action: Show clinical side labels and technical trace separately.

finding: Clinician confirmation is required for medication, injection, imaging follow-up, and referral.
title: Clinician confirmation
why_it_matters: The system supports decision-making but does not issue autonomous orders.
recommended_action: Keep confirmation statement in every report.

finding: Weight loss must preserve muscle when BMI or age suggests risk.
title: Weight-management muscle preservation
why_it_matters: Overly aggressive weight loss can reduce strength.
recommended_action: Pair nutrition with resistance training and monitoring.

finding: Screen distress without attributing structural pain to psychology alone.
title: Psychological screening neutrality
why_it_matters: Neutral language prevents stigma and improves adherence.
recommended_action: Use GAD-7, PHQ-9, PCS, and sleep screening when indicated.

finding: Use current guideline anchors before older evidence for treatment recommendations.
title: Evidence freshness
why_it_matters: Old studies may be background rather than direct prescription anchors.
recommended_action: Show evidence year and guideline anchor status.
